stories.body: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards said they hit U.S. facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, destroyed radar in Oman, and struck Jordan’s Prince Hassan Air Base as Tehran keeps the Strait of Hormuz closed, while Washington reported pounding Iranian air defenses, radars, missile and drone assets, and small boats amid explosions in Bandar Abbas and Qeshm, oil prices jumping above 2%, and dueling claims over whether traffic is moving through the strait as negotiations over a fragile truce falter.
stories.headline: Iran and U.S. trade wider strikes across Gulf states as Hormuz tensions spike

stories.body: Despite Iran’s warning that the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until U.S. interference ends, American naval forces continued escorting commercial shipping through the vital waterway, underscoring the risk of miscalculation as both sides escalate military activity across the Gulf and beyond.
stories.headline: U.S. Navy escorts ships while Tehran insists the Strait of Hormuz will stay shut

stories.body: At a Paris summit, Ukraine joined nine European partners to form the Integrated Anti-Ballistic Missile Coalition, pledging to develop the Freyja system as a lower-cost alternative to U.S. Patriots, license Ukrainian production of French weapons, procure SAMP-T batteries, and stage multinational exercises as Kyiv warns it is running out of interceptors against intensified Russian missile strikes.
stories.headline: Ukraine and allies launch joint anti-ballistic coalition to counter Russian barrages

stories.body: A fire at the Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub in Bangkok killed 27 people and injured 73, with officials citing possible electrical faults, obstructed exits, flammable décor, and beer crates blocking escape routes, prompting renewed scrutiny of Thailand’s spotty enforcement of safety rules at entertainment venues that have suffered deadly blazes before.
stories.headline: Bangkok nightclub blaze kills 27 amid allegations of blocked exits and neglect

stories.body: Typhoon Bavi made twin landfalls in Zhejiang province before weakening inland, toppling trees, flooding streets, canceling thousands of flights and trains, prompting the evacuation of 2.8 million people, injuring 134 in Taiwan, and threatening days of heavy rain across eastern and northern China as authorities warn of landslides and swollen rivers.
stories.headline: Typhoon Bavi batters eastern China and Taiwan, forcing millions to evacuate

stories.body: Sources close to the Kremlin told Reuters that President Vladimir Putin has rebuffed advisers urging a ceasefire, remains determined to seize all of Donbas, and may broaden operations to create a buffer and target NATO-aligned assets, even as Russian fuel shortages, Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries, and domestic discontent grow more acute.
stories.headline: Putin seen preparing escalation in Ukraine despite Western pressure for talks

stories.body: The Trump administration ordered Immigration and Customs Enforcement to halt most vehicle stops after agents fatally shot Colombian national Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine—the second lethal encounter in a week—sparking protests, vigils in the U.S. and Colombia, and demands for accountability as surveillance footage of the incident circulated widely.
stories.headline: ICE suspends most traffic stops nationwide after two deadly shootings in a week

stories.body: A U.S. Hispanic Business Council survey of 1,012 Texas members found one in five lost workers to deportation in the past year and 70% oppose intensified immigration raids, with many former Trump or GOP supporters now backing Democrat James Talarico over Attorney General Ken Paxton as they cite labor shortages, tariffs, and high-profile detentions of community members, including a nun, as reasons for their political shift.
stories.headline: Texas Hispanic business owners turn sharply against Trump over deportation raids

stories.body: Dallas’s bid to showcase itself during the World Cup is colliding with budget shortfalls, furloughed municipal workers, and fierce infighting over whether to raze I.M. Pei’s City Hall for a new arena after the Mavericks and Stars signaled plans to leave downtown, highlighting broader concerns about urban decline, suburban flight, and the leverage sports franchises wield over the city’s weak-mayor government system.
stories.headline: Trump border crackdown strains Dallas as sports politics collide with city finances

stories.body: Republicans in North Carolina are pressing national committees to spend earlier on former RNC chair Michael Whatley, who trails Democrat and ex-governor Roy Cooper in cash and polls, arguing that without major advertising to boost his name recognition the GOP risks losing a must-win Senate seat despite large reserved buys from the Senate Leadership Fund and expectations that new coordination rules will unlock more party money.
stories.headline: North Carolina’s marquee Senate race remains underfunded as GOP frets over Whatley

stories.body: David Sánchez, brother of Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, was sentenced to nine years’ disqualification from public office for administrative malfeasance tied to his 2017 hiring at the Badajoz provincial authority, while former provincial president Miguel Ángel Gallardo received 18 years, though judges dismissed influence-peddling accusations raised by the Manos Limpias union that triggered the 2024 probe.
stories.headline: Spanish court convicts Prime Minister Sánchez’s brother over tailored public job

stories.body: The EU, Spain, the UK, and Gibraltar signed a 336-article treaty that takes effect provisionally on July 15, demolishing the century-old Gibraltar fence, ending routine checks for 15,000 daily cross-border commuters, introducing dual controls at the airport and port, raising Gibraltar’s VAT-equivalent tax to at least 15%, and, according to Spanish officials, inaugurating a “new era” of shared prosperity without altering sovereignty claims.
stories.headline: EU–UK deal dismantles Gibraltar fence and creates joint border controls

stories.body: With Junts siding with PP, Vox, and UPN and Podemos plus Compromís abstaining, Congress rejected the government’s proposed deficit path, forcing Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez to draft 2027 budgets under stricter existing limits and reviving talk that another defeat at the budget’s total vote could trigger early elections, as opposition parties framed the setback as evidence of a collapsing coalition.
stories.headline: Spanish government fails first deficit vote, complicating prospects for 2027 budget

stories.body: A roughly eight-meter-wide sinkhole linked to Line 9 metro construction opened behind properties near FGC Putxet station, forcing the evacuation of residents from eight buildings—about 93 homes—with 67 people rehoused in nearby hotels while firefighters shore up the site, inspect structures, and investigate whether insufficient concrete injections during tunneling caused the collapse reminiscent of the 2005 Carmel cave-in.
stories.headline: Barcelona sinkhole tied to L9 metro works keeps eight buildings evacuated

stories.body: After consecutive summers of record highs above 40°C, Barcelona’s climate office has begun coordinating hospitals, social services, and utilities on a 2027 simulation of 45–50°C heatwaves while expanding shade structures, cool refuges, and low-heat urban materials to strengthen resilience ahead of projections that such temperatures could arrive later this century.
stories.headline: City prepares worst-case heat drill as Barcelona braces for future 50ºC extremes

stories.body: Following an outcry over plans to remove the 1973 signage of Barceloneta’s Bodega Fermín, the city will issue interim guidance allowing a multidisciplinary commission to evaluate storefront graphics for cultural value, while all parties back ERC’s proposal to update the three-decade-old urban landscape ordinance and draft a special protection plan for historic signs, neons, mosaics, and glasswork that help preserve neighborhood identity.
stories.headline: Barcelona moves to protect historic shopfront signage amid tourist churn
